# Asteraid

> Professional web platform for Asterisk PBX administration. Manage multiple Asterisk servers from a single web interface.

Asteraid is a free, self-hosted web platform built by Freeng Ltd for IT engineers who manage Asterisk-based telephony infrastructure. It replaces scattered SSH sessions and manual config editing with a unified, browser-based control center. Every PBX, trunk, and call flow can be orchestrated from one interface with real-time visibility, synchronized control, and harmonic efficiency across your entire network.

## Architecture

The platform runs as two Docker containers: the Configuration Server builds and delivers settings to PBX's over SSH, while MongoDB provides reliable storage for all configurations. The result — a single control point for your entire Asterisk infrastructure.

- Configuration Server: generates configurations, sends them via SSH, serves the web interface
- MongoDB Server: stores all configurations
- Supports deployment on Physical Server, Virtual Machine, or Cloud VM

## How It Works

1. **Installation** — Deploy Asteraid on your server. The step-by-step initialization wizard guides you through all settings in 5 minutes.
2. **Connect PBX's** — Add your Asterisk servers by hostname. Asteraid connects via SSH and starts monitoring automatically.
3. **Set Up Users** — Import employees from Active Directory or create them manually. Assign roles — each gets their own dashboard.
4. **Manage Telephony** — Trunks, extensions, queues, dialplans — all accessible from the browser. The visual routing editor works without Asterisk syntax knowledge.

## Platform Capabilities (20+ Management Modules)

### Dialplan Rule Testing
Simulate a call flow through the dialplan directly from the Visual Dialplan Editor, using the administrator's phone to emulate an incoming call.
- Run call simulation from the editor without leaving the UI
- Use the admin's phone as the emulated incoming call source
- Create a full test environment to debug dialplan logic
- Validate routing rules before applying to production
- Catch errors before they reach live infrastructure

### Disaster Recovery
Everything needed to restore the full configuration of the network of PBXs is stored in a single archive. All you need is a valid backup, the decryption key, and a new infrastructure to start. From new hardware to a fully operational service in just a few minutes.
- Entire PBX network config in one encrypted archive — PBX's, trunks, dialplans, users, certificates
- Restore requires only the backup file and decryption key — no manual reconfiguration
- New hardware → fully operational telephony in minutes, not days
- AES-encrypted backup with centralized key management
- Scheduled automatic backups and one-click full DUMP export

### Version History & VM Boot Mode
Full version history support for the entire configuration. A backup snapshot is created every time the "Apply" button is pressed.
- Automatic snapshot on every configuration apply
- Browse and restore any previous configuration state
- Virtual Machine Boot mode support
- Never lose a working configuration

### Multilingual Support
Multilingual support for voice files at the peer level. Each user can hear IVR prompts and system messages in their own language.
- Per-peer language assignment for IVR prompts and system messages
- Supported languages: English, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Russian, Swedish
- Fully configurable interface language
- Support for custom dictionaries to display GUI elements in any language

### Real-time Monitoring
Via WebSocket the platform continuously receives and displays live metrics from all connected Asterisk PBX's.
- Memory load (RAM)
- Trunk status: online / offline / disabled
- Extension status: online / offline
- Active channels
- Last reload time
- Performance chart (Chart.js)

### Multi-PBX Management
Asteraid lets you add any number of Asterisk servers and manage them centrally.
- Add by hostname / IP
- SSH connection (password or key)
- Timezone and tone code setup
- Maintenance Mode without deletion
- PBX operation log
- Copy SSH key from UI

### Visual Dialplan Editor
Unique feature: build call routing via drag-and-drop block diagram powered by ReactFlow.
- Drag-and-drop blocks: queue, forward, condition
- Visual block connections with arrows
- Generates real Asterisk dialplan
- Preview of resulting code
- Create and edit routing Rules templates

### Call History (CDR)
Full call log via ODBC connection to the CDR database with powerful filters.
- Filter by type (inbound, outbound, missed)
- Search by phone number
- Built-in audio player for recordings
- Export to CSV and XLSX
- Pagination with lazy loading
- Quick access to missed calls

### Extensions, Trunks & Queues
Full management of Asterisk telephony objects: extensions, trunks, templates, queues.
- SIP / PJSIP trunks
- Enable/disable trunk toggle
- Templates for extensions and queues
- Expert configuration (raw config)
- Call queue management

### Users & Roles (RBAC)
Flexible role model with four access levels. Each role gets its own dashboard.
- Administrator: full access
- Supervisor: statistics and groups
- User: own calls and forwarding
- Security: security dashboard
- Phone number assignment
- Switch roles without re-login

### Active Directory Integration
Seamless user import from the corporate LDAP directory.
- Connect to multiple AD controllers
- Map AD groups to Asteraid roles
- Import users with their data
- Show source (AD vs local DB)
- Configure baseDN

### SSH Web Terminal
Full-featured SSH terminal right in the browser powered by xterm.js.
- Connect to any added PBX
- xterm.js — performant terminal
- Clipboard support (ClipboardAddon)
- Auto-resize (FitAddon)
- Fullscreen mode
- Quick launch via URL

### Firewall Management
Built-in firewall at PBX level with ipset support.
- Allow / deny rules by IP / CIDR
- ipset support
- Description per rule
- Applied via Ansible scripts

### Config File Editor
Built-in Monaco editor (like VS Code) for direct Asterisk config file editing.
- Asterisk config file syntax highlighting
- Switch between files
- System and client files
- Fullscreen mode

### SSL Certificates
Certificate management for secure telephony with metadata display.
- Upload certificate and key
- Metadata: subject, issuer, expiry
- Validity status (isValid, hasKey)
- Assign active certificate

### Audit Log
Complete history of all system changes with diff view.
- Action type: create / update / remove
- User who performed the action
- Previous and new value (diff)
- Filter by users and entities

## Role-Based Access Control

4 role-based access levels. Each user sees only what they need for their work.

### Administrator
Full access to all platform sections.
- Summary widgets for all PBX's
- RAM, trunk and extension monitoring
- PBX list with live status
- Performance metrics
- User and role management
- System settings

### Supervisor
Call statistics and team management.
- CDR by period (today / week / month)
- Custom date range
- Charts by number and group
- Employee group management
- Export stats to CSV/XLSX

### User
Personal employee account.
- Own account information
- Assigned phone numbers
- Own call history
- Forwarding settings (CFNA, CFB, CFU, DND)
- View own group

### Security
Specialized security dashboard.
- Unauthorized access attempt monitoring
- Firewall rule management
- SSL certificates and status
- Audit action log
- Encryption key management

### Access Matrix

| Module           | Admin | Supervisor | User | Security |
|------------------|-------|------------|------|----------|
| Dashboard        | ✓     | ✓          | ✓    | ✓        |
| PBX's / Servers  | ✓     | —          | —    | —        |
| Users            | ✓     | —          | —    | —        |
| CDR / Calls      | ✓     | ✓          | ✓    | —        |
| Dialplans        | ✓     | —          | —    | —        |
| Firewall / SSL   | ✓     | —          | —    | ✓        |
| Audit Log        | ✓     | —          | —    | ✓        |
| System Settings  | ✓     | —          | —    | —        |

## Key Advantages

- **Single Portal** — Manage all company Asterisk servers from one place without switching between SSH sessions and different interfaces.
- **Lower Entry Barrier** — Visual dialplan editor and ready-made templates lower the entry barrier — no Asterisk expert needed for every click.
- **Real-time Control** — See infrastructure status here and now — via WebSocket, not cron scripts.
- **Full History** — Audit log records every change: who, what, when and how it was changed. With diff view.
- **Enterprise Integration** — Active Directory, SSL certificates, ODBC CDR databases — full integration with your infrastructure.
- **Security** — Built-in Firewall, TLS certificates, encryption and role-based permissions — security out of the box.
- **SSH in Browser** — No need to open PuTTY. Full SSH terminal right in a browser tab.
- **Scalability** — Works with one PBX and a cluster. Adding new servers takes a couple of clicks.
- **Fast Recovery After Failures** — Automatic configuration backups and built-in recovery tools minimize downtime — even after complete node failure.
- **Zero-downtime Updates** — New versions deploy without restarting Asterisk. Active calls never drop for a second.
- **Peer-to-peer Clustering** — P2P cluster with no single point of failure: every node is equal and autonomous. More reliable than traditional HA stacks.
- **Low Cost of Ownership** — Runs on any Linux server. No specialized hardware platforms or expensive third-party enterprise licenses required.
- **Works on Any OS** — Docker containers provide full portability — Ubuntu, Debian, CentOS, or any cloud without config changes.

## Before vs After: Asterisk Management Comparison

| Task           | Without Asteraid                                                              | With Asteraid                                                                      |
|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Server access  | SSH into each server separately, juggling different IPs and credentials       | All Asterisk servers in one web interface — switch PBX's in one click              |
| Config editing | Edit .conf files in terminal — one typo can break the entire PBX              | Visual editor with templates and Monaco syntax highlighting, no broken configs      |
| Call history   | Raw SQL queries against the CDR database or third-party reporting tools       | Built-in CDR: filters by type/number, audio playback, one-click CSV/XLSX export    |
| Dial routing   | Write Asterisk dialplan syntax by hand — requires deep expert knowledge       | Drag-and-drop visual builder generates real dialplan automatically                 |
| Users & phones | Edit sip.conf manually for every extension change, no bulk operations         | Web UI with templates, bulk CSV import, and Active Directory sync                  |
| Change audit   | No record of who changed what — debugging is a guessing game                 | Full audit log: actor, timestamp, diff view of before/after values                 |
| Firewall & SSL | iptables commands over SSH, manual certificate files, no central view         | Built-in Firewall manager with ipset and SSL certificate dashboard                 |
| Monitoring     | CLI tools (asterisk -r), no dashboards, no alerts                             | Live dashboard via WebSocket: RAM, trunks, extensions, active channels             |

## System Requirements

### Hardware

| Component | Physical Server                             | Virtual Machine                                    |
|-----------|---------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| CPU       | 1 CPU (Intel/AMD x86_64)                    | 1 vCPU x86_64                                      |
| Memory    | 2GB minimum                                 | 2GB minimum                                        |
| Storage   | 16GB minimum                                | 16GB minimum                                       |
| Network   | 1Gb Ethernet adapter (Wi-Fi not supported)  | Ethernet adapter in bridge mode (NAT not recommended) |
| Boot Mode | BIOS or UEFI                                | BIOS compatible                                    |

Note: These are minimum requirements. Production environments typically require more resources. 2 CPU cores and 8 GB of RAM should be sufficient in most cases. Storage figures are for OS with Docker images and containers only — for user data (backups, audio recordings, CDRs) use separate disks.

### Network Requirements
- Stable Internet Connection: Minimum 5Mbps recommended
- DHCP Network: Automatic IP configuration is required
- Firewall Access: HTTP/HTTPS access to Asteraid repositories must be allowed
- Proxy Configuration: Configure proxy settings if behind a corporate proxy

## Technology Stack

### Backend / Server
Node.js + Express, MongoDB, ODBC (CDR), Socket.IO, SSH2, Asterisk AMI, Active Directory (LDAP), FFmpeg, Ansible, JWT + bcrypt, Winston, PM2

### Frontend / Client
React 17 + TypeScript, Redux + Thunk, PrimeReact, ReactFlow, Monaco Editor, xterm.js, Chart.js, Socket.IO Client, React Router v6, driver.js, Luxon

### Deployment
Docker containers (2 containers: Configuration Server + MongoDB)

## Pricing

- **Single-server deployment: Free forever.** No hidden fees. No licenses. Just download and install.
- Multi-server management is a paid feature.

## FAQ

**Does Asteraid work with multiple Asterisk servers?**
Yes, Asteraid is designed for multi-PBX management. You add any number of servers and manage them centrally from one interface.

**Do I need Asterisk knowledge to use the platform?**
Basic knowledge helps, but the visual dialplan editor and configuration templates significantly lower the entry barrier. Most tasks are done through the graphical interface.

**Is Active Directory supported?**
Yes, Asteraid supports connecting to multiple AD controllers with group mapping to roles (Administrator, Supervisor, User). Users are imported with their data.

**How are call records stored?**
Asteraid connects to your existing CDR database via ODBC. Records stay where they always were — nothing is migrated.

**Is there a mobile version?**
The interface is responsive. Primary use is designed for desktop, but key sections are accessible from a tablet.

**How is security ensured?**
JWT authentication, bcrypt password hashing, role-based access control (RBAC), built-in Firewall with ipset support, TLS certificate management, encryption key management.

## Company

Developed and maintained by **Freeng Ltd**. Version 3.1.
